title: cordyline congesta
intro: Cordyline congesta, also known as the cabbage palm or kawakawa, is a plant species native to New Zealand, characterized by its large, palm-like leaves. It has been traditionally used in Maori medicine, exhibiting medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing pain, fever, and inflammation, as well as aiding in digestion and respiratory issues. In traditional Maori herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Cordyline congesta are often used to make infusions, tinctures, and poultices for therapeutic purposes.
